Class Catalog¶

This catalog documents core classes and usage examples.

Database Setup Helper¶

Use setup.sh to initialize PostgreSQL objects required by this project.

What it does:

  • creates database sample if it does not exist

  • creates table dg in sample if it does not exist

Run it:

chmod +x setup.sh
./setup.sh

Prerequisite: PostgreSQL container is running.

docker compose up -d db

QzDistributionGroup¶

class app.dg.QzDistributionGroup(name: str, member: list[str] = <factory>, admin: list[str] = <factory>)¶

Bases: object

Represents a distribution group with members and administrators.

admin: list[str]¶
classmethod from_db_row(row: tuple[str, list[str], list[str]]) → QzDistributionGroup¶

Create an instance from a database row.

Parameters:

row – Tuple ordered as (name, member, admin).

member: list[str]¶
name: str¶
to_db_tuple() → tuple[str, list[str], list[str]]¶

Convert the instance into a tuple matching the dg table columns.

Examples¶

Create and print a group:

from app.dg import QzDistributionGroup

group = QzDistributionGroup(
    name="Engineering Updates",
    member=["alice@example.com", "bob@example.com"],
    admin=["lead@example.com"],
)
print(group)

Convert to database tuple and reconstruct:

db_row = group.to_db_tuple()
reconstructed = QzDistributionGroup.from_db_row(db_row)
print(reconstructed)

PostgreSQL Python Starter
